content: Instead of scaling working memory capacity to improve computer automation agent reasoning, we can adapt the agent's automation task decomposition to fit its native working memory limit, turning a fixed constraint into a task structuring principle. This means an automation agent limited to holding 5 active context items should split large workflows into a sequence of 5-step sub-tasks, rather than constantly offloading and retrieving context from external long-term memory.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
